name: Suite 2 — Aggregation (COUNT / SUM / AVG / MIN / MAX / GROUP BY / HAVING)
description: Single-row aggregations, COUNT DISTINCT, group-by with multi-column grouping, and HAVING clauses. Tests the QuerySpec planner's ability to emit empty select[] for pure aggregates and proper groupBy when categorical columns are present.

Scenarios.Question: how many tickets
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: count of active users
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Count FROM AspNetUsers WHERE IsActive = 1

Scenarios.Question: how many open tickets
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE s.Name = 'Open' AND t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: number of tickets created this month
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets WHERE CreatedAt >= DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(GETDATE()),MONTH(GETDATE()),1) AND IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: how many distinct ticket statuses
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT Id) AS DistinctStatuses FROM TicketStatuses

Scenarios.Question: how many unique users have created a ticket
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT CreatedByUserId) AS DistinctCreators FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: average resolution time in days
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT AVG(CAST(DATEDIFF(DAY, CreatedAt, ResolvedAt) AS FLOAT)) AS AvgResolutionDays FROM Tickets WHERE ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: mean ticket age in days
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT AVG(CAST(DATEDIFF(DAY, CreatedAt, GETDATE()) AS FLOAT)) AS AvgAgeDays FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: earliest ticket creation date
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT MIN(CreatedAt) AS EarliestCreatedAt FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: latest resolved ticket date
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT MAX(ResolvedAt) AS LatestResolvedAt FROM Tickets WHERE ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: ticket count by status
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT s.Name, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY s.Name ORDER BY Count DESC

Scenarios.Question: ticket count by priority
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT p.Name, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY p.Name ORDER BY Count DESC

Scenarios.Question: ticket count by status and priority
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT s.Name AS Status, p.Name AS Priority, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY s.Name, p.Name ORDER BY Count DESC

Scenarios.Question: ticket statuses with more than 50 tickets
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT s.Name, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY s.Name HAVING COUNT(*) > 50 ORDER BY Count DESC

Scenarios.Question: priorities with at least 10 tickets
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT p.Name, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY p.Name HAVING COUNT(*) >= 10 ORDER BY Count DESC
